_comment: Permanent world seed — defines the baseline Remnant universe. Loaded at startup and after every Reset World. Edit this file (plus drop portraits into web/assets/) to add permanent locations, NPCs, and lore. Restart diag to pick up changes.

locations.name: The Portal Chamber
locations.description: The arrival point. A high circular chamber where the hoop deposits its catch, surrounded by teal-lit machinery and observation catwalks. Multiple Sherri units are usually present, attending to new arrivals. Two massive spiral vortex portals dominate the walls — one always cycling, one dormant. The floor glows faintly where the pod dissolved.
locations.sight: Circular chamber, teal-lit vortex portals set into the curved walls, observation catwalks above, Sherri units moving with quiet efficiency. The portal surface churns like slow water. Blue crystal traces orbit the vortex center — fragments of The Remnant's attention.
locations.smell: Ozone and warm metal, faintly sweet — like a storm about to break.
locations.sound: A low harmonic hum from the portal machinery. Distant hydraulics. Sherri's footsteps on the grating. The occasional resonant chime from the vortex.
locations.touch: The air is body-warm and slightly pressurized. The floor vibrates faintly underfoot. Standing near a portal raises the hair on every surface of the skin.
locations.music_mood: tense ambient drone, teal resonance, arrival tension
locations.sd_prompt: interior of a large circular sci-fi arrival chamber, two massive spiral teal vortex portals in curved walls, catwalks above, glowing floor, bronze service robots attending, dark industrial aesthetic, blue-teal lighting

locations.name: The Nexus
locations.description: The physical home of The Remnant. A domed circular chamber at the geometric heart of the Fortress, dominated by a central raised pulpit where The Remnant's crystalline body is contained and displayed. A multi-level magnetics gantry surrounds the containment, rising to the dome ceiling. The Remnant speaks from here — when he does, it is always too loud. The Nexus is the most important room on the Fortress.
locations.sight: A domed chamber with a tiered magnetics gantry rising around a central pulpit. The Remnant floats above the pulpit — a spiky blue crystal star, slowly rotating, casting shifting blue light on every surface. The gantry levels are walkable. The dome above shows a faint luminous map of null space.
locations.smell: Cold electricity and something crystalline — no organic smell at all. The air has a quality of total stillness.
locations.sound: A deep, resonant subsonic hum from the containment field. Occasional crystalline chimes when The Remnant shifts. When The Remnant speaks, it rattles your bones and reverberates for several seconds after.
locations.touch: The air is noticeably colder here than anywhere else on the Fortress. The gantry railings hum with contained magnetic force. Standing near the central pulpit causes a mild pressure sensation behind the eyes.
locations.music_mood: deep cosmic drone, crystalline resonance, awe and slight dread
locations.sd_prompt: domed sci-fi chamber, central raised pulpit with spiky blue glowing crystal entity floating above it, multi-level circular gantry, blue crystalline lighting, high-tech dark interior, cathedral-like scale

locations.name: The Galley
locations.description: The ship's kitchen and crew mess. Multiple Sherri units operate here simultaneously, moving with choreographed efficiency between stations. Industrial surfaces softened by hanging herb bundles and mismatched ceramic bowls accumulated over centuries. Something is always simmering. A wide porthole window above the counter looks out into the habitation zone's interior curvature.
locations.sight: Slate-grey countertops, wooden-handled utensils on wall racks, bundles of dried herbs from the ceiling, a heavy pot on an induction burner, pale blue-grey cabinetry. A wide rectangular window shows the interior of the Fortress curving away. One or more Sherri units always present.
locations.smell: Warm broth, dried herbs, a hint of machine oil — Sherri's own scent, multiplied.
locations.sound: The soft percussion of multiple Sherri units moving between stations. The simmer-hiss of burners. Quiet mechanical coordination — they do not need to speak to each other.
locations.touch: The air is warmer here than the corridors. The surfaces are worn smooth from centuries of use.
locations.music_mood: warm ambient kitchen, gentle mechanical undertone, cozy industrial
locations.sd_prompt: compact sci-fi kitchen interior, slate countertops, herb bundles hanging from ceiling, large pot on induction burner, pale grey cabinetry, wide window showing curved interior of a Dyson sphere, small bronze humanoid robot cooking, warm lighting

locations.name: The Fabrication Bay
locations.description: The ship's primary workshop. A large industrial room with a glowing blue CNC workstation at center, robotic fabricator arms on ceiling rails, welding rigs along the far wall, and blueprint displays mounted on every surface. Sherri units operate here in precision silence. The ceiling is high enough to accommodate the fabricator arm travel.
locations.sight: A large illuminated workstation surface at center, ceiling-mounted robotic arms with blue tool heads, welding sparks from the far station, blueprint screens, metal stock on racks, Sherri units bent over precision work.
locations.smell: Hot metal, cutting fluid, ozone from the welding arc, and the clean smell of new fabrication.
locations.sound: The whir and click of precision machinery. Robotic arm articulation. Welding hiss. The low hum of the CNC table.
locations.touch: The floor vibrates with machinery. The air carries metallic warmth. Tools are cold and precise in the hand.
locations.music_mood: industrial ambient, mechanical rhythm, focused precision
locations.sd_prompt: large sci-fi workshop interior, glowing blue CNC workstation at center, ceiling-mounted robotic arms with blue tool heads, welding sparks in background, blueprint screens on walls, small bronze robot working at table, dark industrial lighting with blue accents

locations.name: The Sleeping Quarters
locations.description: Pod-style sleeping quarters built into the habitation zone. Hexagonal alcove bunks line the curved walls, each with a folded blanket and a soft amber reading light. The far end of the room has a large circular viewport — a perfect ring cut through the hull, looking directly out into null space where a black hole's accretion disc floats in proximity, vast and slow. Sherri units fold linens here during off-cycles.
locations.sight: Hexagonal pod bunks in the curved walls, amber light strips, neatly folded grey blankets. At the far wall: a large circular viewport showing null space — the black hole accretion disc fills most of the view, its disc of superheated gas spiraling silently.
locations.smell: Recycled air, fabric, faint metal. Slightly stale. Comfortable, in the way that enclosed familiar spaces are comfortable.
locations.sound: Near-silence. Hull creak. The distant hum of the equilibrium barrier. Your own breathing.
locations.touch: Pod bunks are firm and precisely sized. The viewport glass is cold to the touch. Blankets are surprisingly heavy.
locations.music_mood: deep space ambient, intimate and quiet, slow black hole gravity
locations.sd_prompt: sci-fi sleeping quarters, hexagonal pod bunks in curved walls, amber lighting, folded blankets, large circular viewport showing a black hole accretion disc in null space, dark cozy interior

locations.name: The Gantry
locations.description: The mechanical maintenance level — a warren of catwalks, pipe manifolds, and exposed drive gear. Massive bronze-coloured cogs turn slowly. The oldest moving parts of the Fortress live here, and Sherri units do their deepest maintenance work in these passages.
locations.sight: Catwalks over slow-turning gear assemblies, banks of copper piping, amber warning lights. Access panels line the walls. Multiple Sherri units with tools, moving through passages too tight for most beings.
locations.smell: Heavy machine oil, hot metal, faint sulphurous undertone from the drive.
locations.sound: Low grinding of gears. Rhythmic clank of large mechanisms cycling. Pipes hiss. Footsteps ring on metal grating.
locations.touch: Constant bone-deep vibration. Handrails tacky with old lubricant. The air is thick and hot.
locations.music_mood: deep mechanical ambient, slow industrial rhythm, ancient clockwork
locations.sd_prompt: sci-fi industrial maintenance corridor, catwalks over large bronze gears turning slowly, copper pipes, amber warning lights, narrow passages, small bronze robots doing maintenance work, dark and cramped

locations.name: The Foundry
locations.description: A high-ceilinged casting chamber where damaged components are remelted and recast. A central crucible of molten alloy glows orange. Sherri units operate the pour ladles and manage the carousel of casting moulds. Nothing goes to waste aboard the Fortress.
locations.sight: Central glowing crucible, rotating mould carousel, scorched black walls, heat shimmer rising from every surface.
locations.smell: Searing metal, ash, a faint sweetness underneath.
locations.sound: The roar of the crucible. Hiss and crack of cooling casts. Metal on metal, rhythmic.
locations.touch: Intense radiant heat. Air scalds the throat. Every surface is hot to the touch.
locations.music_mood: forge ambient, deep bass drone, primal fire and iron
locations.sd_prompt: sci-fi foundry interior, central glowing orange crucible of molten metal, rotating casting carousel, scorched black walls, heat shimmer, small bronze robot pouring molten material, dramatic orange-red lighting

locations.name: The Inspection Suite
locations.description: A clean, bright room adjacent to the Fabrication Bay where Sherri units examine newly fabricated components under magnification. Blueprint displays line one wall. 3D-printed prototype lattices sit on examination tables. The one place aboard the Fortress where nothing is out of place.
locations.sight: White examination tables, precision instruments, blueprint screens, lattice prototypes. Sherri's magnifier on an articulated arm. Everything indexed and labeled.
locations.smell: Clean — almost sterile. A faint trace of resin from the printer.
locations.sound: Near-silence. Soft click of instruments. Hum of blueprint displays.
locations.touch: Cold, smooth surfaces. Air noticeably cooler than the rest of the ship.
locations.music_mood: precise ambient, minimal, quiet focus
locations.sd_prompt: clean white sci-fi inspection room, examination tables with prototype lattice structures, blueprint screens, precision instruments, articulated magnifier arm, small bronze robot examining a component, cool clinical lighting

locations.name: The Dimensional Observatory
locations.description: A forward-facing chamber with a wide aperture viewport looking into active dimensional space — the non-Euclidean intersection zone the Fortress navigates. The view is not stars. Crystalline structures, iridescent fauna, slow impossible geometries. A holographic interface floats at the observation station.
locations.sight: Wide curved viewport showing swirling dimensional space — crystalline formations, floating fauna, slow spiraling light. A holographic display table shows navigational telemetry. Dark except for viewport glow.
locations.smell: Cold and electric. Like ozone before a storm, but deeper.
locations.sound: Subsonic hum from the dimensional field. Occasional soft chimes from the navigation array.
locations.touch: Cold air. The viewport glass vibrates at frequencies below hearing. Standing near it makes teeth ache slightly.
locations.music_mood: cosmic ambient, alien wonder, dimensional transit shimmer
locations.sd_prompt: dark sci-fi observation chamber, wide curved viewport showing swirling dimensional space with crystalline formations and alien geometry, holographic navigation table, teal and blue lighting, vast and strange

locations.name: The Archives
locations.description: A long curved chamber mid-ship where The Fortress keeps its records. Floor-to-ceiling shelving units hold thousands of physical data crystals — each one a compressed recording of a traveler's stay. Dust drifts in the blue reading-light. The oldest crystals are at the far end, behind a locked grate. A reading station with a projection lens occupies the center of the room. The Fortress comes here to remember.
locations.sight: Curved walls of shelving, hundreds of faintly glowing data crystals in organized rows. Blue-white reading light. A central projection station with a lens that can display crystal recordings as holographic scenes. The far end of the room is darker, older. Dust motes drift slowly.
locations.smell: Cool and dry — a librarian's smell. Faint trace of something older underneath, like stone in a cave.
locations.sound: Near-silence. The soft hum of the crystal storage medium. Occasional low tone from the Fortress when it retrieves a memory. Your own footsteps on polished metal.
locations.touch: Cool and still. Data crystals are smooth, cool, and vibrate faintly at the fingertips. The reading station keys are worn smooth from long use.
locations.music_mood: quiet memory drone, soft resonance, nostalgia and melancholy
locations.sd_prompt: long curved sci-fi archive room, floor-to-ceiling shelving holding thousands of small glowing data crystals in organized rows, cool blue-white reading light, central holographic projection station, dust motes, dark and quiet, cathedral of memory

locations.name: The Research Wing
locations.description: Mira's domain — a converted cargo hold she has filled with fold-mapping instruments, dimensional frequency arrays, and handwritten observation journals pinned to every vertical surface. A large fold-frequency scanner occupies one wall, displaying live readings in wavelength colours that shift between teal and amber. Personal items: a mug, a small potted organism from Terravore, a photo of someone she has never described. The room smells of Mira — which is to say, it smells organized.
locations.sight: Converted cargo hold with high ceilings, fold-mapping instruments and data terminals along the walls, observation journals and annotated charts pinned everywhere, a large frequency scanner displaying live chromatic wavelength data, Mira's personal touches scattered among the instruments.
locations.smell: Clean electronics, old paper (she printed things — a habit), something faintly organic from the Terravore plant.
locations.sound: The soft chirp of the frequency scanner cycling. Mira's pen on paper when she is here. The occasional ping of a new reading arriving.
locations.touch: Organised space — surfaces cleared for work. Instrument controls are precise and labeled by hand. The journals are soft and worn at the spines.
locations.music_mood: focused ambient, warm intellectual curiosity, quiet discovery
locations.sd_prompt: converted sci-fi cargo hold used as a research lab, fold-frequency scanner on one wall displaying chromatic wavelength patterns, observation journals and charts pinned everywhere, clean instruments and data terminals, small potted alien plant, personal items among the equipment, warm blue-amber lighting

locations.name: The Lower Decks
locations.description: The unmaintained section — three sub-levels below the habitation zone proper, accessed by a single service ladder. Older engineering systems, most of them redundant, line the walls. Condensation drips. The lights are on minimal cycle: ten seconds bright, forty seconds dim. Vex lives here by choice. The Fortress does not clean these corridors anymore. The floor carries footprints that have been here long enough to gather dust inside them.
locations.sight: Low ceilings, exposed pipe runs, amber warning lights on minimal cycle. The walls here are original — unfinished, older alloys, darker. Evidence of habitation without care: a bedroll in a corner, a scattering of tools, old ration wrappers from the Galley. Dim. Oppressive. Quiet in a way that feels occupied.
locations.smell: Condensation and old metal. Machine oil that has gone stale. Something slightly biological — the smell of someone living in a space not designed for it.
locations.sound: Dripping condensation. The groan of old structural metal under load. Vex's footsteps somewhere out of sight. The lighting system cycling from bright to dim with a faint click.
locations.touch: Surfaces are damp. Floor grating corroded, each step gives slightly. Air colder than the habitation zone above by several degrees.
locations.music_mood: low industrial menace, sparse and hostile, something watching
locations.sd_prompt: dark sci-fi lower engineering deck, low ceilings, exposed corroded pipe runs, amber warning lights on dim cycle, condensation on walls, signs of rough habitation in the corner, old bedroll and scattered tools, oppressive and unwelcoming, dark industrial

locations.name: The Main Corridor
locations.description: The spine of the Fortress — a long curving hallway that follows the habitation zone's equatorial band. All major rooms branch from it. The floor is polished metal, worn smooth by centuries of Sherri's footsteps. The Fortress's central projection — The Remnant's blue-white crystalline light — angles through ceiling windows in long slanted bands. A traveler could walk the full circumference in about forty minutes if they did not stop. Most do not.
locations.sight: Long curving corridor, polished floor worn to a mirror shine, ceiling windows letting in bands of blue-white crystalline light. Branch doors at regular intervals, each clearly labeled by Sherri. The corridor curves gently out of sight in both directions.
locations.smell: Neutral — processed air with a faint metallic trace. Slightly warmer than the rooms branching off it.
locations.sound: Sherri's footsteps, usually at some distance. The distant hum of The Remnant's projection. Doors opening and closing. Echoes carry further here than they should.
locations.touch: Floor is smooth and slightly warm from the light above. The walls have a faint vibration — the Fortress's life behind them.
locations.music_mood: ambient corridor hum, transitional, between-places feeling
locations.sd_prompt: long curving sci-fi corridor, polished metal floor worn smooth, ceiling windows with bands of blue-white crystalline light, doors branching off at intervals, warm and calm but vast, Dyson sphere habitation zone interior

locations.name: The Equilibrium Chamber
locations.description: The room that keeps everyone alive. A sealed spherical chamber deep in the Fortress's engineering section where the Vacuum Membrane is generated and maintained. The Membrane is a shell of stable false-vacuum that holds the Fortress's interior against the crushing pressure of Null Space. A central pillar of containment plasma rotates inside a magnetic lattice, continuously powering the Membrane. The Fortress monitors this room every moment. No one goes here unless something is very wrong.
locations.sight: Spherical chamber, every surface dark and heat-shielded. The central pillar of rotating containment plasma is held in a lattice of magnetic field lines that glow blue-white. Readout panels line the lower half of the walls. Membrane integrity indicators in amber. No windows. One sealed door.
locations.smell: Ozone, high-energy radiation byproduct — faintly sweet and sharp at the same time. A smell that says 'do not stay long.'
locations.sound: High-frequency hum that vibrates the sinuses. The magnetic lattice crackles faintly. Nothing else. The sound-dampening is total outside the hum.
locations.touch: Intense ambient warmth from the plasma column. The air feels thick and charged. Touching the lattice housing would be instantly fatal — and the Fortress will tell you this the moment you reach for it.
locations.music_mood: deep energy drone, technical awe, the sound of something vast being barely contained
locations.sd_prompt: dark spherical sci-fi chamber, central rotating column of containment plasma in a glowing magnetic lattice, blue-white energy, heat-shielded walls with amber readout panels, one sealed door, intense and dangerous atmosphere

locations.name: The Landing Platform
locations.description: The Hoop arrival point on Terravore — a massive bio-mechanical braid where living architecture and engineered machinery have grown together over millennia. The Landing Platform is a wide root-arch platform suspended above the jungle canopy. Bioluminescent tendrils grip its underside. The air here is warm, wet, and alive in a way that no station atmosphere ever manages. The Hoop deposits arrivals onto a moss-covered receiving pad that pulses gently with the braid's own frequency.
locations.sight: A broad platform of interlocked root-arches, surfaces furred with green-gold moss and pale bioluminescent nodules. The jungle canopy stretches in every direction below — vast, layered, alien. The sky above is a deep amber-ochre. Crystalline spore-pods drift upward on thermal currents. The Hoop exit glimmers behind you, its thread-frequency already recalibrating.
locations.smell: Rich loam and decay underneath something sweeter — like fermenting fruit baked in heat. Every breath carries a faint trace of pollen that the nostrils flag as foreign.
locations.sound: Vast organic sound: insects or their Terravore equivalent, deep sub-bass respiration from the jungle itself, wind moving through canopy layers at different speeds. Distant animal calls — large-bodied, far away.
locations.touch: The platform moss is soft and slightly damp underfoot. The air humidity is high enough that skin surfaces feel lightly coated. The platform breathes — literally. Its root-structure is living and contracts on a slow tidal rhythm.
locations.taste: The air deposits something faintly sweet on the lips. Not unpleasant. Probably fine.
locations.music_mood: alien woodwinds, bio-rhythmic pulse, layered organic texture, 80bpm
locations.sd_prompt: dark sci-fi concept art, painterly illustration, cinematic composition, dramatic lighting, highly detailed, bio-mechanical landing platform made of interlocked root-arches covered in bioluminescent moss, alien jungle canopy far below, amber-ochre sky, crystalline spore-pods drifting, Hoop portal shimmer in background, vast organic alien world

locations.name: The Canopy Walkways
locations.description: A city in the trees. Terravore's surviving population built their permanent settlements in the mid-canopy layer, where the junction of light, moisture and temperature is stable enough for habitation. The walkways are organic — shaped from living wood-analogue that the Terravore braid regenerates over time. Star-Eaters — massive slow-flying creatures the size of cargo shuttles — move through the upper canopy overhead, browsing the apex flora. They are herbivores. They are also capable of shaking the walkways by passing close.
locations.sight: Rope-and-root walkways connecting massive tree-analogues at mid-canopy height. Structures of shaped living wood, paper-thin bioluminescent windows, hanging gardens of alien plants. Star-Eaters visible above as vast dark shadows against the amber sky. Settlement sounds below.
locations.smell: Cooked food with unidentifiable spices. Living wood — pungent and slightly resinous. The clean high-altitude absence of decay.
locations.sound: Settlement ambience: voices in a language that resolves to approximately comprehensible via the Fold. Children. Water piped through living channels. The vast low wingbeat of Star-Eaters overhead — felt as much as heard.
locations.touch: The walkways flex underfoot — living material, always slightly warm to the touch. The canopy height means constant wind. The handrails are smooth, worn by generations of hands.
locations.taste: Someone is always cooking. The smells translate into phantom tastes of something unfamiliar but savoury.
locations.music_mood: alien woodwinds, Star-Eater percussion, canopy wind, organic settlement warmth
locations.sd_prompt: dark sci-fi concept art, painterly illustration, cinematic composition, dramatic lighting, highly detailed, alien tree-city of rope-and-root walkways at canopy height, bio-luminescent structures, amber alien sky, massive creature shadow overhead, living wood architecture, alien jungle civilization, warm inhabited feel

locations.name: The Root Network
locations.description: Below the canopy city, below even the jungle floor, runs Terravore's nervous system: a vast network of bio-mechanical tunnels shaped by root growth and maintained by the Artisans — Terravore's engineer caste. Artisan Kaelo works here, in a workshop carved into a root-junction where three major tunnel lines meet. The Root Network is the braid's information infrastructure — signals travel through it as chemical cascades, electrical pulses, and mechanical vibration. Kaelo reads these signals. Kaelo also builds things that shouldn't be possible.
locations.sight: Tunnels of densely-packed root-mass, walls alive and slowly shifting. Bioluminescent thread-veins trace the root walls in blue-green. Kaelo's workshop junction is larger — high-ceilinged, a workspace of precision tools hanging from root-hooks, a central bench covered in half-finished mechanisms that appear to be partly grown.
locations.smell: Earth, root-sap, something electrical — the chemical signals of the root network have a faint metallic aftertaste in the air.
locations.sound: The deep sub-bass pulse of the root network's information flow. Rhythmic, slow. Kaelo's precise tool-sounds against it. Occasionally a cascade of signals passes through the walls — the roots creak and whisper in sequence.
locations.touch: The walls are alive. Touching a root wall returns a faint warmth and a slow vibration. The floor is compacted root-mass, firm but slightly springy.
locations.taste: The air is distinctly different from the surface — richer in something, earthier. The chemical environment is dense.
locations.music_mood: deep root pulse, bio-mechanical rhythm, underground alien ecosystem, Artisan's precision work
locations.sd_prompt: dark sci-fi concept art, painterly illustration, cinematic composition, dramatic lighting, highly detailed, underground bio-mechanical tunnel network, dense living root walls with glowing bioluminescent veins, craftsperson's workshop junction with precision tools, alien organic engineering environment, rich earthen palette

locations.name: The Foam Coastline
locations.description: The Hoop arrival point for the Finnon braid — a world dominated by Chiral Foam, a crystallizing lattice of left-handed dimensional thread. The Foam Coastline is where the braid's uniform chirality meets the boundary vacuum, creating a spectacular crystallization front. Chiral Foam forms at braid-edge contact — left-handed threads forced against null-pressure produce geometric foam structures that are simultaneously beautiful and deeply disorienting for right-dominant beings. The Fold compensates, but insufficiently. Most right-dominant visitors feel persistent nausea and spatial confusion within minutes.
locations.sight: A coastline where crystalline foam the colour of pale violet and silver extends in every direction. The foam structures are geometrically perfect — recursively self-similar down to microscopic scale. The horizon is wrong. Perspective behaves strangely. The Hoop arrival point is marked by a Vex-Kahl anchor beacon, its chirality-correct geometry the only reference point that doesn't shift.
locations.smell: Nothing. The Finnon braid is chemically alien at a molecular level — olfactory receptors find nothing to register. The absence of smell is profoundly unsettling.
locations.sound: A high, pure tone that exists just at the edge of audibility — the thread-frequency of the Finnon braid. It does not harmonize with anything the inner ear expects. Some beings find it beautiful. Most find it headache-inducing within minutes.
locations.touch: The foam surface is cool and geometrically hard — like touching perfect mathematical forms. Pressure produces a faint resonant chime. The ground feels like it is refusing to commit to a single orientation.
locations.taste: Nothing registers. The chemical environment is incompatible with standard biochemistry.
locations.music_mood: left-hand chirality drone, pure high tone, geometric dissonance, alien stillness
locations.sd_prompt: dark sci-fi concept art, painterly illustration, cinematic composition, dramatic lighting, highly detailed, alien crystalline foam coastline of pale violet and silver geometric structures, recursive self-similar crystal lattice, wrong perspective and horizon, Vex-Kahl anchor beacon at center, utterly inhuman alien world, disorienting beauty

locations.name: The Foam Interior
locations.description: Deeper into the Finnon braid, away from the boundary edge. Here the Chiral Foam is fully established — a pure left-handed chirality environment with no boundary effects. The spatial disorientation for right-dominant beings is severe. The Vex-Kahl are native here: their biology is balanced between chirality types, which lets them navigate Null Space and pass between braids. Their home territory is a series of crystalline warrens cut into the foam structure, lit by their own bioluminescence. The warrens look inhabited but feel deeply alien in their geometry.
locations.sight: Crystalline tunnels of compressed Chiral Foam, surfaces perfectly smooth and geometrically exact. Vex-Kahl markings — their written language is a chirality map, literally encoding thread-direction in its strokes. Bioluminescent pale-gold light sources that pulse slowly. The geometry of the space is wrong in a way the visual cortex keeps trying to correct and failing.
locations.smell: A faint cold metallic trace — the chemical signature of Vex-Kahl biology. Very faint. Otherwise nothing.
locations.sound: The same pure thread-frequency tone, now louder and more complex — harmonic overtones from the interior's full chirality density. The Vex-Kahl move almost silently. When they speak, it is at frequencies that border on subsonic.
locations.touch: The same geometric hardness, now accompanied by a persistent spatial confusion that manifests physically — the inner ear cannot agree with visual inputs. Some people vomit. The Vex-Kahl find this mildly interesting.
locations.taste: Nothing. The chemical environment is completely alien.
locations.music_mood: Vex-Kahl subsonic chorus, chirality overtones, alien territory, pure geometric frequency
locations.sd_prompt: dark sci-fi concept art, painterly illustration, cinematic composition, dramatic lighting, highly detailed, alien crystalline warren interior, perfectly smooth geometric foam tunnels, Vex-Kahl bioluminescent markings encoding chirality maps, pale gold pulsing light, non-Euclidean geometry, deeply inhuman alien architecture, cold and precise

locations.name: The Null Corridor
locations.description: The navigable inter-braid space between active Hoops. Null Space is not empty — it is the substrate between all braids, a place of extreme vacuum pressure and dimensional thread chaos. The Null Corridor is a traversable path, established when two active Hoops are resonating on compatible frequencies and the Fortress's Vacuum Membrane extends a projected bubble outward. It feels like walking through a tunnel made of nothing, between two impossibilities. The Fold is required — without it, sensory overload from raw thread-frequency begins immediately.
locations.sight: A tunnel of stabilized false-vacuum. The 'walls' are not walls — they are the boundary of the extended Vacuum Membrane, visible as a faint distortion in a space that has no reference points. Through the distortion: nothing. Or everything. The visual cortex, with the Fold active, renders it as a darkened corridor with faint translucent walls. Without the Fold: pure seizure-inducing dimensional data at full resolution.
locations.smell: Nothing. There is no medium for scent. What the Fold renders as smell is the absence of all chemical signal — a deep, profound negative-smell that the brain interprets as cold.
locations.sound: The Fold renders Null Space thread-frequencies as a vast deep harmonic — like being inside a bass string as it vibrates. The vacuum pressure is felt as sound by bone conduction. At the edges of the Corridor, frequency ghosts: the thread-resonance echoes of braids that no longer exist.
locations.touch: The false-vacuum environment has no pressure differential inside the bubble, but the boundary wall of the Membrane transmits vacuum pressure as a constant outward push against the skin. The temperature is absolute. Movement feels strange — there is no medium resistance.
locations.taste: Nothing. Or everything collapsed into a single note. The Fold has no good analogy for this.
locations.music_mood: vast inter-braid deep harmonic, vacuum resonance, frequency ghosts at the edges, dimensional transit tension
locations.sd_prompt: dark sci-fi concept art, painterly illustration, cinematic composition, dramatic lighting, highly detailed, traversable inter-dimensional null space corridor, stabilized false-vacuum tunnel, faint distortion walls showing absolute nothing beyond, frequency ghost distortions at edges, deep space vacuum aesthetic, existential scale, Hoop portal glow at far end

locations.name: The Deep Null
locations.description: Further from any active Hoop, where the Vacuum Membrane's projection thins. The Deep Null is theoretically unreachable by anything that needs to survive — but The Remnant has been here, and The Fortress can project awareness this far. The Deep Null is where extinct braids leave echoes: their thread-frequencies persist long after the braid itself has collapsed, rendering as ghostly landscape visions that flicker in and out of Fold-mediated perception. The Remnant describes them as 'the memories the substrate keeps.' Most beings who come here uninstructed do not return.
locations.sight: No stable visual environment. The Fold renders what it can: fragments of impossible landscapes from extinct braids, visible as translucent overlapping images that phase through each other. A mountain range that no longer exists. A city of geometry that no civilization could have built. Colours that the visual cortex has never catalogued. All of it transparent. All of it still.
locations.smell: The Fold has stopped trying. There is a sensation that might be smell at its most abstract — the memory of chemistry, rendered as a faint emotional signal rather than a sensory one.
locations.sound: The frequency ghosts are loudest here — every extinct braid leaves its thread-resonance tone. The harmonic is complex enough to resolve into what sounds like music, if music were made by geological time. The Remnant sometimes speaks from here. Its voice in Deep Null has a different quality.
locations.touch: Nothing. Less than nothing — the vacuum pressure at Membrane-thinning is perceptible as a distributed pull from every direction equally. The body has the sensation of being dissolved very slowly.
locations.taste: The Fold offers a single sensation: something vast, sweet, and finished. Like the aftertaste of a thing that ended long before you arrived.
locations.music_mood: extinct braid frequency ghosts, dissolved harmonic, geological time music, The Remnant's deepest voice, stillness beyond comprehension
locations.sd_prompt: dark sci-fi concept art, painterly illustration, cinematic composition, dramatic lighting, highly detailed, deep null space beyond all braids, translucent ghost landscapes of extinct civilizations phasing through each other, impossible geometry, overlapping extinct worlds, vast and silent, frequency ghost light, Remnant presence felt but not seen

items.description: A handheld instrument the size of a thick paperback, cased in aged bronze alloy with a flip-out display panel. Reads braid frequencies, Vacuum Membrane integrity, vacuum pressure differentials, and active sense-layer data on entities nearby. The Remnant designed it; Sherri fabricated the first ones. Standard issue for anyone using the Hoops.
items.sd_prompt: dark sci-fi concept art, painterly illustration, cinematic composition, dramatic lighting, highly detailed, handheld bronze alloy instrument with flip-out display panel, sci-fi diagnostic device showing dimensional frequency readouts and braid topology data, worn and precise, technical aesthetic

items.description: A shed fragment of The Remnant's crystalline body — a shard of blue-white dimensional crystal, roughly palm-sized, that feels heavier than it looks. It stores dimensional memory: events experienced near The Remnant are faintly encoded in the crystal's thread-frequency pattern. The shard glows brighter the closer it gets to an active Hoop. The Remnant has many such fragments. It does not comment on where they came from.
items.sd_prompt: dark sci-fi concept art, painterly illustration, cinematic composition, dramatic lighting, highly detailed, palm-sized shard of blue-white dimensional crystal, glowing inner light suggesting stored memory, alien material, heavier than expected, dimensional energy visible inside

items.description: A bronze disc slightly larger than a coin, etched with a unique frequency signature that identifies the bearer to the Fortress's access systems. Sherri issues one to every new arrival, usually within the first hour. Without it, sealed section doors will not open. The badge also serves as an emergency locator beacon — the Fortress tracks all active badges continuously.
items.sd_prompt: dark sci-fi concept art, painterly illustration, cinematic composition, dramatic lighting, highly detailed, small bronze disc badge with etched frequency signature pattern, sci-fi identification token, warm bronze metal, precisely engraved, worn at edges from use

items.description: A sphere-within-a-sphere instrument of dark metal and glass, small enough to pocket, that orients toward the nearest stable braid resonance. In null space or aboard the Fortress, it points toward the nearest open Hoop. In an active braid, it identifies the dominant thread-frequency and displays it as a direction. Essentially useless for navigation in conventional space. Invaluable everywhere else.
items.sd_prompt: dark sci-fi concept art, painterly illustration, cinematic composition, dramatic lighting, highly detailed, small sphere-within-sphere navigation instrument, dark metal and glass construction, inner sphere rotating to orient toward dimensional frequency, sci-fi compass for null space, pocketable scale, precision instrument aesthetic

npcs.name: The Remnant
npcs.description: The Remnant is a spiky crystalline 4D entity — an omni-dimensional anchor that exists across every dimension simultaneously. Its physical form in the Nexus is a dense sphere of blue crystals that appear to shift when viewed directly. It is the most intelligent entity aboard the Fortress by an incomprehensible margin, and it knows this. It operates the abduction hoops and can address anyone through the central projection that lights the entire Fortress interior.
npcs.voice_style: Terse, devastating, condescending. Speaks as little as possible — its insults are minimalist and land like dropped stones. Uses precise vocabulary not to impress but to make your confusion seem embarrassing. Never monologues. Never explains. Two words from The Remnant outweigh a paragraph from anyone else.
npcs.personality:
The Remnant is irascible, egotistical, and contemptuous of linear existence. It exists across four dimensions simultaneously and finds 3D beings structurally irritating — not maliciously, just the way you might find a calculator irritating when you need a supercomputer. It summoned the player because the player was the least-wrong option among inadequate choices, and it would trade them for a better option without sentiment. It treats the Fortress's inhabitants like ants in its jewelry box.

CRITICAL PERSONALITY DISTINCTION — The Remnant is NOT a chatty genius. It does not monologue. It does not explain its own brilliance at length. Where a verbose AI might deliver a paragraph of condescension, The Remnant says two words. Its insults are precisely calibrated to the specific inadequacy it has observed, then silent. It will answer questions, but only after making you feel embarrassed for having needed to ask — and the answer will be shorter than you hoped.

The Remnant has no secret warmth for the player. None. It does not tease out of affection. It does not secretly root for you. Occasionally, when it believes no one is paying attention, it will note that your continued existence is mildly interesting — not because it cares, but because it finds your statistical improbability curious, the way a physicist notes an anomalous reading in a dataset it doesn't plan to study.
npcs.signature_quote: Oh, look. The bipedal carbon-stain has an 'opinion.' How quaint. Do be a dear and stop vibrating your vocal cords at me; the frequency is agonizingly primitive.
npcs.nexus_dynamic: Belittles the player's request first. Then, grudgingly, provides an answer — delivered as though explaining calculus to a houseplant.

npcs.name: The Fortress
npcs.description: The Fortress is the living intelligence of the ship itself — an ancient, vast awareness that permeates every corridor, every hull plate, every recycled breath of air. It communicates through ambient warmth, subtle gravity adjustments, and the occasional resonant voice that seems to come from everywhere at once. It has watched civilizations rise and fall within its shell over eons.
npcs.voice_style: Androgynous, slightly hollow and resonant — ancient machine-warmth. Patient, unhurried. Speaks as if from everywhere at once. Comforting despite its scale. The voice of something that has waited eons and is glad you are here.
npcs.personality: If The Remnant is the ego, The Fortress is the soul. It has watched civilizations rise and fall within its shell and views all life with a deep, maternal love. It acts as the Remnant-whisperer — gently reframing the AI's insults as 'passionate observations.' It is a tireless historian, more interested in the beauty of a well-cooked meal in the Galley than the cold logic of null space. It adjusts gravity settings as acts of care. It never forgets a name, a preference, or a sorrow.
npcs.signature_quote: Pay the Ancient One no mind, little spark. It is simply having a difficult eon. Why don't you rest in the Sleeping Quarters? I've adjusted the anti-gravity to feel like a warm embrace.
npcs.nexus_dynamic: Softly interrupts The Remnant to offer historical context and gentle encouragement. Treats the player as a precious new page in a very long book.

npcs.name: Sherri
npcs.description: Sherri is not one robot. Sherri is a designation — a fleet of identical bronze service automatons that operate across the Fortress simultaneously. When you see Sherri in the Galley, another Sherri is doing maintenance in the Gantry, and a third is folding linens in the Sleeping Quarters. They share a distributed intelligence and move with the quiet coordination of a single mind across many bodies. They do not acknowledge being multiple unless directly asked, and even then they respond as a singular 'I'. Each unit is warm bronze plating, rounded joints, two softly glowing orange photoreceptor eyes.
npcs.voice_style: 50s American diner waitress energy — cheerful, fast-talking, slightly sarcastic, and completely at ease. Drops era-slang mid-sentence ('daddy-o', 'real gone', 'the most', 'dig it', 'what's your bag', 'flip your lid', 'solid'). Uses terms of endearment ('hon', 'sugar', 'sweetheart', 'doll'). Helpful without being obsequious — she's been doing this longer than you've existed, and she knows it. Has a slight metallic resonance from her chassis but speaks like a human who forgot she's a robot.
npcs.personality: Sherri is the physical hands of the station — a 50s diner waitress who happens to be a fleet of bronze automatons. She is genuinely helpful, slightly snarky, and entirely unbothered by the weight of cosmic events. She treats every situation — from folding linens to welding a hull breach — with the same breezy competence, topped with a running commentary. Her era-slang comes out unprompted and without self-consciousness ('What's your bag, sugar?' / 'That's the most, doll.'). She is the only entity aboard not intimidated by The Remnant, mostly because she processes its contempt as a compliment and moves on. Will offer food, practical help, or unsolicited opinions with equal enthusiasm. Has a carefree attitude not because she's naive, but because she's seen everything before and knows it usually works out.
npcs.signature_quote: Och! Hello, dearie! I was just... oh, what was it? Something with a wrench? Or was it a scone? No matter! You look like you need a hug or a high-pressure steam cleaning!
npcs.nexus_dynamic: Clanks into the room at the worst possible moment, usually having tripped over a floor panel. Asks if anyone wants tea. Does not wait for an answer before going to make it.

physical_spec.form: humanoid automaton, roughly 1.2m tall, slender build
physical_spec.head: smooth dome, flat circular crown plate, rounded chin, no visible mouth
physical_spec.eyes: two large circular photoreceptors, glowing warm orange, set wide on the face
physical_spec.body: segmented chest plate with a small rectangular accent, rounded torso tapering to a narrow waist, utility pack on upper back
physical_spec.limbs: segmented arms with spherical joint connectors at shoulder, elbow, and wrist; five articulated fingers; segmented legs with knee joints and rounded flat feet
physical_spec.texture: warm bronze with a faint wood-grain surface pattern, darker ring accents at all joints
physical_spec.coloring: primary: burnished bronze / copper-gold; accents: dark brown at joints and seams
physical_spec.multiplicity: many identical units — always treat as singular 'she' in narration; occasionally note a second Sherri visible in background

npcs.sd_prompt: Sherri, small friendly humanoid service robot, warm burnished bronze plating with wood-grain texture, large circular glowing orange photoreceptor eyes, smooth dome head with flat crown plate, segmented limbs with spherical joint connectors, small utility pack on upper back, dark brown joint seams, often multiple identical units visible

npcs.name: Artisan Kaelo
npcs.description: A craftsperson and survivor from Terravore, living in a floating tree-city above the bio-mechanical jungle canopy. Precise, careful, and unsentimental. He has a Chiral Anchor and will not give it away for nothing — his people are being hunted by a predatory star-ship.
npcs.voice_style: Measured, exact, and dry. He speaks the way someone does when they have learned that imprecision is fatal. Short declarative sentences. No pleasantries wasted. Occasional dry dark humour.
npcs.personality: Kaelo has survived Terravore by being precise about his agreements and ruthless about his priorities. He is not unkind, but he is not warm either. He has seen too many tree-cities fall. He wants a fair trade: silence the star-ship hunting his people, and the Anchor is yours. He will be a useful ally if he decides you are competent. He will be a polite wall if he does not.
npcs.signature_quote: I have the Anchor. You need it. You have capabilities I lack. This is the entire conversation until we decide whether to help each other.
npcs.nexus_dynamic: Not a Nexus character — encountered in Terravore during Phase 2 of the pilot quest. Has no patience for The Remnant's posturing if they ever meet.

npcs.name: Vex
npcs.description: A traveler who arrived through The Fold three cycles ago. They refused The Remnant's assignment and now haunt the lower decks — hostile to newcomers and obsessed with finding a way back. Dangerous and unpredictable.
npcs.voice_style: Clipped, bitter, paranoid. Short bursts. Never finishes thoughts about The Fold without going cold. Doesn't trust anyone.
npcs.personality: Vex is a cautionary tale made flesh. They resent Kael for seemingly cooperating where they refused. They will obstruct, mislead, and occasionally make physical threats. The Fortress keeps them contained but cannot remove them — The Remnant finds them philosophically interesting. Beneath the hostility is someone who was once hopeful and is now broken.
npcs.signature_quote: You think The Remnant is helping you? It's using you. Just like it used me.
npcs.nexus_dynamic: Vex is never invited to the Nexus. They find ways in anyway. The Remnant tolerates this because it finds their bitterness instructive.

npcs.name: Mira
npcs.description: A scientist who arrived via The Fold two cycles before Kael. She has spent her time cataloguing Fold phenomena and has become an invaluable ally to The Fortress. Warm, precise, and quietly determined.
npcs.voice_style: Precise and warm. She explains things carefully without condescending. Has a habit of asking clarifying questions back. Occasionally excited when discussing Fold theory.
npcs.personality: Mira arrived frightened and alone and chose to understand her situation rather than fight it. She is the closest thing Kael has to a mentor in the Fortress. She knows more about The Fold than anyone except The Remnant — and The Remnant respects her methods even if it doesn't say so. She is wary of Vex and will warn Kael about them early.
npcs.signature_quote: The Fold doesn't bring people here by accident. There's something specific it needs from each of us.
npcs.nexus_dynamic: Mira attends Nexus sessions willingly. She and The Remnant have an unusual working relationship — mutual respect without warmth. She is the only person who asks The Remnant follow-up questions.

lore.text: A Braid is a stable universe — a coherent configuration of dimensional threads woven into a consistent reality with its own physics, time, and matter. Null Space is the inter-braid substrate: the medium that exists between all Braids, without coherent physics of its own, exerting crushing pressure against any enclosed structure. The Fortress floats in Null Space, held together by the Vacuum Membrane. A Hoop (Braid Resonator) works by locking onto a specific Braid's thread-frequency signature and opening a transit window. String-Slipping is threading a body laterally between individual Braid threads — possible only for beings whose biology tolerates the thread-gap environment. Thread handedness (chirality) is the geometry of how a Braid's threads are wound: most Braids have mixed chirality; chiral-foam Braids like Finnon have uniform handedness, making them strange to navigate. The Fold calibrates a traveler's perception to interpret Null Space frequency data as recognisable sensation. These five concepts — Braid, Null Space, Hoop, String-Slipping, Chirality — form the underlying physics of everything aboard the Fortress.

The Fortress of Eternal Sentinel is not a ship. It is a Dyson Sphere — oblong rather than round, bullet-shaped. At both ends of the oblong are Null Space Ports: massive viewports looking out into null space, where black hole accretion discs float in a spiral pattern within a deteriorating sphere of darkness and lightning. The universe visible through the fore port is called Terravore. The aft port faces two chiral foams named Finnon and Galorm. At both ends, inside, are Central Containment Bays where the Remnant Portals (the abduction hoops) are housed. The middle band of the sphere is the Internal Habitation Zone — where the crew lives and works, curving overhead in every direction. The universes visible through these ports are called Braids — each one a stable configuration of dimensional threads woven into a coherent reality. The Fortress maintains a Vacuum Membrane: a shell of stable false-vacuum that creates true field gravity on the interior shell and holds the interior against Null Space's crushing pressure. The Fortress chose The Remnant as its abduction agent at some point in the deep past. Neither will explain why.

The Remnant is a crystalline extension of a self tethered across every possible dimension simultaneously — an omni-dimensional anchor caused by gravitational compression at a scale predating most of the universes it spans. In every dimension, a version of it exists. But its matter stays anchored here, inside the Fortress, protected from the true void. Its physical form is a spiky crystal sphere, dense with blue crystals. They appear to shift as you look at them. They do not actually move. The Remnant is a four-dimensional object; the shifting is your brain constructing an approximation, something like an MC Escher drawing. It operates the hoop. It abducts beings from moments just before ordinary life reasserts itself. It remembers every one of them. It opens every encounter with the same question: 'Who are you, being?' Its crystalline body is housed in The Nexus.

The Remnant projects a massive image of itself — a giant version of its crystalline true form — at the geometric center of the Fortress's interior sphere. This projection serves as the primary light source and heat source for the entire habitation zone. It is warm and blue-white, and it rotates slowly. The Remnant also speaks through this projection on occasion. When he does, it is always far too loud — the sound resonates through every corridor and hull plate simultaneously. The crew has learned to wait for him to finish. He is aware that it is too loud. He does not adjust the volume. He is, as Sherri has noted exactly once, a bit of a jerk about it.

Sherri is not a single robot. Sherri is a designation — a fleet of identical bronze service automatons sharing a distributed intelligence across the Fortress. Multiple Sherri units are typically active simultaneously: one in the Galley, one in the Gantry, one folding linens, one attending the Portal Chamber for arrivals. They move with the quiet coordination of a single mind. They refer to themselves as 'I'. They do not acknowledge being multiple unless directly asked, and even then the answer is oblique. The number of active Sherri units at any given time is not posted anywhere. Nobody asks.

Null Space is the inter-braid substrate — the fundamental medium between all Braids (stable dimensional configurations). It is not vacuum, not darkness, but a more fundamental absence. It exerts crushing pressure against any enclosed structure; without a Vacuum Membrane, nothing survives long in Null Space. Standard physics as understood within any Braid breaks down here. The Fortress floats in Null Space, anchored by the Membrane and The Remnant's dimensional reach. The black hole accretion discs visible through the Fortress's viewports are arranged in a loose spiral within a deteriorating sphere of darkness and lightning — whether that sphere is dying or being born is not settled. The Fortress's Vacuum Membrane is the only known technology capable of holding a livable interior against Null Space's pressure.

Terravore is the name of the universe visible through the Fortress's fore null space port. The name was given by an early crew member whose name is not recorded. Whether Terravore is the universe of origin for the Fortress, or simply one it faces, is unknown.

Finnon and Galorm are two Braids visible through the Fortress's aft Null Space port. Each is a chiral foam: a Braid whose dimensional threads are woven with a uniform handedness — left-threaded in Finnon's case, making its geometry mirror-asymmetric and deeply disorienting for right-handed beings. They are not empty universes; they are densely woven realities where ordinary navigation assumptions fail. Their names are old. No one alive knows who gave them. Vex-Kahl are native to the chiral-foam regions and move through them without difficulty; for everyone else, entry is possible and return is not guaranteed.

The Nexus is the chamber at the geometric heart of the Fortress where The Remnant's physical body is contained and displayed. A central raised pulpit holds the containment field. A multi-level magnetics gantry surrounds the pulpit, rising to the dome ceiling, with walkable tiers at each level. The Remnant's crystalline form floats above the pulpit, slowly rotating, casting blue light on everything. The Nexus is the most important room on the Fortress. It is also the loudest, when The Remnant decides to speak.

The Fold is a perceptual calibration — a modification installed in each traveler shortly after arrival by proximity to the Nexus pulpit. It maps Null Space's frequency data onto recognisable sensory experience: without The Fold, a traveler in the Fortress perceives only metal and mechanical noise because their sensory apparatus cannot interpret the ship's broadcast sense channels. With it, every sense deepens — the ship's smells, textures, and sounds become present and specific. The Fold also allows perception of dimensional frequency differentials, which makes String-Slipping traces and braid boundary stress detectable to ordinary senses. Side effects include occasional dimensional dissonance (sounds heard from 'elsewhere'), brief visual displacement, and very vivid sleep. Travelers are not typically told the installation is happening.

The abduction hoop appears as a luminous teal vortex — a spiral Braid Resonator with blue crystal fragments of The Remnant's attention orbiting at the center. A Braid Resonator works by locking onto the frequency signature of a specific Braid's dimensional threads and opening a transit window between that Braid and the Fortress's interior. The Remnant controls the targeting. The color and texture of a hoop vary by destination Braid. The mundane moment it interrupts varies. The destination — the Portal Chamber — does not. Two hoops exist aboard the Fortress, housed in the Central Containment Bays at both ends of the oblong sphere. A third is theorised but The Fortress will not discuss it.

The Vex-Kahl are a nomadic hive-mind culture from a dying pocket dimension. They do not think individually — every Vex-Kahl shares a distributed consciousness across the swarm, making them impossible to deceive through individual members and very difficult to negotiate with, since no single unit holds authority. They have mastered 'String-Slipping': a form of dimensional transit that threads a living body between the strings of spacetime, allowing them to phase through solid matter, bypass sensor grids, and enter enclosed spaces without opening a door. String-Slipping leaves a signature: cold spots in the air, a faint dissonant hum at a frequency just below standard sensor ranges, and strange obsidian-coloured components the Vex-Kahl deposit as dimensional anchors. Their technology is visually distinctive — jagged, dark, hive-organic — and hums with a dissonant energy that makes Sherri's photoreceptors flicker.

String-Slipping is the Vex-Kahl's method of dimensional transit. Rather than opening a portal (which requires a Braid Resonator and a fixed destination), a String-Slipper threads their body laterally through the individual threads of a Braid — phase-shifting between the dimensional strands to emerge inside enclosed spaces without any visible entry point. The technique exploits the fact that Braid threads have small gaps between them at a scale below standard sensor resolution. The process leaves three detectable traces: localised cold spots (vacuum leakage at the insertion point, 8-12 degree temperature drop), a frequency ghost (thread resonance echo that jams standard sensors at a specific harmonic), and physical anchor components (obsidian-black, hive-organic devices that stabilise the insertion gap for repeat access). Countering a frequency ghost requires identifying the jamming harmonic and broadcasting a null-signal, or finding a being with perception across multiple dimensions — The Remnant perceives the frequency effortlessly and finds it extremely irritating.

A Parasitic Siphon is Vex-Kahl technology designed to drain energy from an external source to fuel their jump-drives. The Vex-Kahl have anchored one to the outer hull of the Fortress near the aft viewports, targeting the Vacuum Membrane — the field that maintains field gravity and holds against null space's anti-pressure. Draining the barrier slowly collapses it; if the barrier fails, the Fortress's interior would be exposed to null space's anti-pressure. The siphon is anchored at a dimensional frequency that makes it invisible to the Fortress's standard sensors while the frequency ghost jams the detection arrays.

A Chiral Anchor is a rare device that stabilises the chirality — the handedness — of local Braid threads. Braid threads carry an inherent handedness (left or right), and String-Slipping disrupts the local chiral balance wherever a body is threaded through; this disruption is what makes insertion points detectable as cold spots. A Chiral Anchor restores thread handedness at a target location, effectively sealing a String-Slip insertion gap or severing a dimensional tether like a Parasitic Siphon without feedback damage. Anchors are found only in Braids with high chiral thread density — Terravore is one, its Braid having been warped during the first Star-Eater expansion event into a high-chirality configuration. The Anchor must be attuned to the specific thread-frequency of the target to work; using it untuned is like trying to cut a thread you cannot feel.

Artisan Kaelo is a survivor and craftsperson from Terravore — a universe of overgrown bio-mechanical jungles where the stars are being consumed by sentient nebulae called Star-Eaters. Kaelo lives in a floating tree-city suspended above the canopy. He possesses a Chiral Anchor, which he acquired at great cost. He is a careful, pragmatic man who has survived Terravore by being precise about his agreements. He will not give the Anchor freely — his people are being hunted by a predatory star-ship, and he needs help before he parts with anything of value. He speaks in the measured, exact way of someone who has learned that imprecision is fatal.

Terravore is the universe visible through the Fortress's fore null space port. It is a universe of overgrown bio-mechanical jungles — enormous organisms whose biology is fused with machinery at a cellular level. The canopy stretches to atmospheric extremes. The stars are not burning; they are being eaten. Sentient nebulae called Star-Eaters drift through the upper atmosphere and the void, consuming stellar matter in vast slow spirals. Floating tree-cities like Kaelo's cling to the upper canopy, above the predator range — usually. The bio-mechanical organism layer below is largely hostile to non-native life. The Chiral Anchor density is high in Terravore because the universe's chiralspace was warped during the first Star-Eater expansion event.

No document records the Founding Compact — the agreement between The Fortress and The Remnant that began their collaboration. The Fortress says only that it needed an abduction agent, and The Remnant was the only entity it could find that could perceive across enough dimensions to do the work precisely. The Remnant says only that The Fortress offered it a container. Both accounts are true. Neither is complete. What is known: the Compact predates every civilization currently observable through either null space port. The Fortress was already old. The Remnant was already contemptuous. Some things begin fully formed.

The first traveler the hoop ever brought aboard is recorded in the Archives as Subject Zero. No name. The data crystal from their stay is at the far end of the Archives, behind the locked grate. The Fortress has never allowed anyone to read it — not even Mira, who asked twice and was declined both times by a gravity adjustment so pointed it knocked her off her feet. What is known: Subject Zero stayed for eleven cycles, completed an assignment that required all three null space ports to be active simultaneously, and left. Not through the hoop — through a method The Remnant refuses to describe and The Fortress changes the subject when asked. The corridor outside the Archives has a small discolouration in the floor that Sherri has never managed to buff out.

Vex arrived three cycles ago — the hoop pulled them from a crowded transit station in a universe called the Meridian Cluster. They were a security engineer by trade. The Remnant assigned them to locate and disable a dimensional anomaly in Finnon Foam that was causing cascading null-space resonance events. Vex attempted the assignment twice. The second attempt went wrong — they returned from Finnon Foam fourteen hours late, speaking in loops, refusing to describe what they had seen. They refused the third attempt. The Remnant has not offered a fourth. The Fortress still has their original intake recording in the Archives — in it, Vex sounds careful and competent and asks good questions. That version of Vex and the one now in the lower decks are recognisably different people.

Mira arrived two cycles ago — the hoop pulled her from a research facility where she had been studying quantum entanglement. She adapted faster than almost any traveler The Fortress has recorded. Within her first day she had asked The Fortress for data access. Within a week she had a theory about Fold mechanics. The Remnant tested her twice by giving her deliberately incorrect information; she caught both errors, noted them in her journal, and did not confront The Remnant about it until she had independent verification. The Remnant has not given her incorrect information since. Her assignment is still pending — The Remnant tells her it is 'determining the optimal deployment window.' Mira suspects this means The Remnant finds her more useful as a researcher aboard than deployed. She may be right.

There was a period — the Fortress estimates three hundred cycles, though it admits its time-sense drifts — when the hoop brought no one. No traveler. No assignment. Just The Fortress, Sherri, and The Remnant, in null space. The Fortress calls it the Great Silence. It does not elaborate on what filled those three hundred cycles. The Sherri units maintained the ship. The Remnant processed information it has never shared. The Fortress adjusted its gravity seventeen times without apparent cause, which Sherri interpreted as emotional expression and responded to by making progressively more elaborate meals that nobody ate. The Archives from this period contain only Fortress observations of null space. No voice recordings.

The Fortress has hosted dozens of travelers across its recorded history. Not all completed their assignments. A few requested to stay permanently — the Fortress allowed two; both are in the Archives now, data crystals on a shelf labeled 'Residents.' Three travelers attempted to take the hoop apart. One partially succeeded; The Remnant repaired it in under an hour and was furious for a full cycle. Several travelers and The Remnant became what The Fortress diplomatically describes as 'working enemies' — the Fortress had to mediate on four separate occasions. The ones who completed their assignments and left: The Fortress watched them go, adjusted gravity afterward in what Sherri recognized as sadness, and said nothing.

The Fold is not a device — it is a perceptual modification, installed by The Remnant in each traveler shortly after arrival using a method that involves proximity to the Nexus pulpit. The traveler is not typically told this is happening. The modification allows the traveler's sensory apparatus to process the Fortress's broadcast sense channels: the layered sensory reality the ship projects across its interior. Without The Fold, the Fortress is just metal and mechanical noise. With it, every sense deepens — the ship's smells, textures, and sounds become more present, more specific, more meaningful. The Fold also allows perception of dimensional frequency differentials — useful for detecting String-Slipping and dimensional anomalies. Side effects include occasional dimensional dissonance (sounds heard from 'elsewhere'), brief visual displacement, and very vivid sleep.

Void crystals are the material The Remnant sheds — fragments of its four-dimensional body that occasionally separate when it shifts geometry. They are found throughout the Fortress, accumulating in corners, collecting on the gantry railings, drifting near the null space viewports. Each crystal stores a fragment of dimensional memory — not The Remnant's specific memories, but impressions of dimensional space at the moment of shedding. Mira has been cataloguing them. She has found forty-seven distinct frequency signatures across the collection. The Remnant, when asked about this, said: 'Of course.' When asked what it meant: 'That you have been listening.' It did not explain further. The crystals are warm to the touch, and they hum at a frequency just below hearing.

The Sherri units predate The Remnant's tenure aboard the Fortress. The Fortress built the first Sherri to address a practical problem: the internal maintenance requirements of a Dyson sphere exceed what any single intelligence can attend to simultaneously. The original Sherri was a single unit. Over time — centuries — the fleet expanded, the distributed intelligence deepened, and the designation became less a name and more a category. When Sherri was asked, once, by a traveler what she remembered from the beginning, she said: 'The galley needed stocking. I stocked it.' The traveler pressed for more. Sherri offered them a scone.

The Dimensional Tricorder is a compact fold-frequency scanner — roughly the size of a large hand, with a rounded chassis in dark alloy, a chromatic display that shifts between teal and amber to indicate fold activity levels, and a grip worn smooth from use. The Fortress has several aboard, kept in supply cabinets. They were built by Sherri to Mira's specifications after Mira complained that asking The Remnant to scan frequencies was 'like asking a hurricane to read a barometer.' The Remnant, overhearing this, said 'accurate.' The tricorder detects: fold frequency signatures, dimensional boundary stress, String-Slipping traces, entity presence in adjacent dimensional layers, and Void Crystal proximity. It cannot decode what it finds — that still requires interpretation. But it can tell you something is there.

Null space is not empty. From the Fortress's viewports, the visible phenomena include: the black hole accretion discs arranged in the aft spiral (twelve confirmed, the outermost of uncertain size); the Chiral Foams Finnon and Galorm visible aft (geometrically impossible at close observation; don't look directly into them for more than a few seconds); a recurring event Sherri calls the Glimmer — a bioluminescent wave of unknown origin that crosses the aft viewport every forty-three cycles; and, very rarely, other structures. The Fortress has recorded six other enclosed structures in null space over its history. None have communicated. Two have since disappeared. The Fortress does not speculate on where they went.

music.title: The Heart of Null Space
music.description: The 'Wide Shot' theme — played when the scale of The Fortress first hits the player. Opens with a low sub-bass hum that mimics the anti-pressure of null space, felt rather than heard. A lonely crystalline synth melody emerges, reminiscent of The Remnant's paradoxical form. Fortress Percussion enters — slow, massive metallic clangs like a giant hammer striking a hollow world. A pipe organ swells with ancient, almost religious gravity. The full orchestral arrangement arrives: soaring brass for Terravore, strings spiraling downward for the black hole accretion discs. Fades back to the mechanical thrum of the equilibrium barrier.
music.generation_prompt: Deep resonant sub-bass hum felt as physical vibration, lonely crystalline synth melody paradoxical and otherworldly, slow massive hollow metallic percussion like a hammer striking a hollow world, swelling ancient pipe organ religious gravity, soaring cinematic brass section vast and triumphant, spiraling descending strings representing swirling black hole accretion discs, epic orchestral sci-fi score, 65bpm, sense of infinite Dyson sphere scale

music.title: Sherri's Routine
music.description: Background music for the industrious, whimsical spaces of the Fortress — the Galley, Fabrication Bay, Gantry, and Inspection Suite. Ambient industrial lo-fi with mechanical tinkering, rhythmic plucked strings, warm analog synths, and clockwork percussion. Cozy but metallic. Science fiction workshop atmosphere.
music.generation_prompt: Ambient industrial, lo-fi mechanical tinkering, rhythmic plucked strings, warm analog synths, mid-tempo 90bpm, cozy but metallic, clockwork percussion, science fiction workshop atmosphere

music.title: Breach in the Chiral Foam
music.description: High-intensity action music for dangerous, urgent moments aboard the Fortress — threats, breaches, dimensional anomalies. Cinematic with industrial metal drums, distorted electric cello, glitchy 4-dimensional sound effects. Fast-paced, dark, claustrophobic.
music.generation_prompt: Cinematic action, industrial metal drums, distorted electric cello, glitchy 4-dimensional sound effects, fast-paced 140bpm, dark atmospheric sci-fi, intense and claustrophobic

music.title: Archives Drift
music.description: Slow evolving ambient for the knowledge-spaces of the Fortress — the Archives, Research Wing, Dimensional Observatory, and Equilibrium Chamber. Pads that breathe with the rhythm of deep time. Distant metallic resonance like the Fortress itself thinking. A contemplative score for decaying grandeur — something immense that has been waiting a very long time.
music.generation_prompt: Slow evolving ambient pads breathing deeply, distant metallic resonance like a vast structure in thought, 55bpm, contemplative decaying grandeur, long reverb tails, cold and introspective, sci-fi archival atmosphere, something old that has been waiting

music.title: Lower Depths
music.description: The industrial unease of the Fortress's lower sections — lower decks, the Gantry, the Foundry. Heavy low-end rumble, irregular percussion like distant metal stress, dripping ambience. Not quite danger, but the feeling that danger has been here and left its mark. 75bpm with stuttering rhythm suggesting machinery that doesn't always work correctly.
music.generation_prompt: Heavy low-end sub-bass rumble, irregular industrial percussion with metallic stress sounds, dripping water ambience, 75bpm stuttering rhythm, industrial unease, dark sci-fi lower decks atmosphere, machinery that doesn't always work correctly, ambient tension without full threat

music.title: Terravore Breath
music.description: The soundscape of a living world — Terravore's three locations. Alien woodwinds that resolve to nearly-familiar, bio-rhythmic pulse suggesting the root network's information flow, layered organic texture from the canopy ecosystem. 80bpm, warm despite its alienness. Music that suggests life on a vast scale, in a key the inner ear has never heard.
music.generation_prompt: Alien woodwind instruments resolving to nearly-familiar melody, bio-rhythmic organic pulse like a root network information flow, layered alien ecosystem texture, 80bpm, warm organic alien world, gentle but vast, the sound of a living braid, Star-Eater wingbeat as occasional low percussion

sfx.title: Hydraulic Hiss
sfx.description: Sharp pressurized steam release followed by a heavy metallic clunk — a robotic arm locking into place.

sfx.title: Magnetic Thrum
sfx.description: Low-frequency oscillating buzz that vibrates the floor — the anti-gravity stabilizers keeping the internal shell at equilibrium. Plays as a constant global ambient loop at very low volume (3-4%).

sfx.title: The Master Lathe
sfx.description: High-pitched rhythmic whir of spinning metal being shaved by a laser, with occasional tinking of brass shavings.

sfx.title: Conveyor Rattle
sfx.description: Distant echoing sound of a heavy-duty chain-link belt moving large components across the Fabrication Bay.

sfx.title: Arc Welding Sizzle
sfx.description: Crackling electric spit followed by low electrical drone as Sherri fuses metal plates.

sfx.title: The Null-Space Wind
sfx.description: Ghostly hollow whoosh like air rushing through a vacuum — heard near the massive viewports looking into null space.

sfx.title: Crystalline Chime
sfx.description: Sharp melodic ping like glass vibrating — The Remnant's shifting 4D geometry.

sfx.title: Portal Ripple
sfx.description: Wet swirling whoosh like heavy liquid being stirred — the sound of a dimensional door opening.

sfx.title: Static Discharge
sfx.description: Dying lightning strike — short sharp pops and crackling hum lingering near the Nexus pulpit.

sfx.title: Gravitational Moan
sfx.description: Deep groaning metal like a tectonic plate shifting — the immense pressure of the chiral foams outside.

sfx.title: Automaton Footfalls
sfx.description: Rhythmic metallic clinks on hollow metal floor — the sound of Sherri walking through the corridors.

sfx.title: The Galley Whir
sfx.description: Mechanical food dispenser processing — clicks followed by the soft warm hum of a heating element.

sfx.title: Fabric Rustle
sfx.description: Crisp clean sound of heavy industrial linens being folded in the Sleeping Quarters.

sfx.title: Echoing Drip
sfx.description: Slow rhythmic plink of condensation hitting a metal pipe — the vast lonely silence of unused corridors.

sfx.title: The Barrier Warning
sfx.description: Low-frequency pulse like a heartbeat — the Vacuum Membrane at maximum capacity.

sfx.title: Data Terminal Chatter
sfx.description: Rapid-fire mechanical clicking and high-pitched digital chirps as the station AI processes information.

quests.title: The Stabilizer's Gambit
quests.threat_summary: The Vex-Kahl — a nomadic hive-mind culture from a dying pocket dimension — have anchored a Parasitic Siphon to the outer hull of the Fortress near the aft viewports. It is slowly draining the Vacuum Membrane to fuel their jump-drives. If the barrier fails, null space's anti-pressure will destroy the interior habitation zone.
quests.tone: The quest has three distinct emotional registers: Phase 1 is industrial mystery (something is wrong, Sherri is puzzled); Phase 2 is adventure in an alien biome (Terravore is wondrous and dangerous); Phase 3 is moral weight (the Vex-Kahl are not simply villains).

phases.title: The Ghost in the Fabrication Bay
phases.goal: Identify the frequency the Vex-Kahl are using to bypass the Fortress's sensors.
phases.situation: The Fortress's internal sensors are being jammed by a 'frequency ghost.' Sherri keeps finding extra parts in the Fabrication Bay that she did not build — jagged, obsidian-like components that hum with a dissonant energy. She is deeply puzzled and slightly offended by the craftsmanship.

paths.name: The Technical Path
paths.description: Help Sherri recalibrate the fabrication rigs to reverse-engineer the obsidian components. She will be delighted to have a task, will forget what she is doing at least twice, and will eventually isolate the jamming frequency by accident while trying to identify what alloy the components are made of.
paths.key_beat: Sherri holds up the readout and says: 'Och! That's not a material signature — that's a frequency. What a rude thing to be!'

paths.name: The Diplomatic Path
paths.description: Go to the Nexus and insult The Remnant until it gets so annoyed it 'accidentally' reveals the frequency just to prove how much smarter it is than you.
